problem_summary: 2021 MCM/ICM Problem E (sustainability): design a national food-system model that can evaluate efficiency, profitability, sustainability, and equity; apply it to at least one developed and one developing country; show what happens (and how long it takes) when the system is re-optimized toward equity and sustainability; analyze the benefit/cost trade-off of shifting priorities; and discuss scalability/adaptability. This team ("Less Hunger & More Peace", #2119031) decomposed the food system into three sequential sub-models — production, transportation, sales — then defined three evaluation indicators on top of them, and wrapped everything in a single weighted multi-objective optimization solved by genetic algorithm. Case study: India (developing) and France (developed), plus a 10-country generalization and a Somalia stress test.
models_used: Model I — Food Production Process Model: additive source decomposition P_{i,t}=A (plantation: Yield*Area summed over crops) + B (animal husbandry: (Meat+Milk+Egg)*Num) + C (fishery: Fish*FishArea), summed over regions to a national total

Calorie-correction sub-model: converts physical mass output to a common calorie basis using per-food ratio lambda with 100g rice = benchmark 1 (rice 1, maize 1.009, sorghum 1.189, beef 1.123, pork 1.299, chicken 0.337, silver carp 0.300, shrimp 0.268); lets hunger be measured in calories rather than tonnage

Model II — Food Transportation Process Model: carrier-profit model. Freight cost SC = k1*w*l + k2*l (fuel proportional to weight*distance, labor proportional to distance); acquisition cost AC = sum P*rho*ac; revenue QB = sum Q*qb; per-trip profit TP = QB(dest) - AC(origin) - SC

Transportation Selection Algorithm Driven by Profit Optimization (Algorithm 1): greedy agent-based simulation — abstract each 'living area' into one transporter; for each food and each timestep, each origin area ships to whichever destination maximizes TP, building allocation matrix U^p over time

Model III — Food Sale Process Model: supply-demand differential equation for price, d(qb)/dt = (supply - demand)*alpha with qb(t0)=p0; demand = beta*pop - phi*qb (population-driven, price-elastic, low elasticity since food is a necessity)
Per-capita income model: individual incomes drawn i.i.d. from a Normal distribution N(mu_{i,t}, sigma^2) to allocate food-purchasing power

People Food Selection Algorithm (Algorithm 2): agent-based purchase simulation — rich agents buy randomly until calorie need met; poor agents prioritize high-calorie-per-cost foods until need met or money runs out; FAO 1800 kcal/day threshold flags hungry individuals into hunger matrix H

Indicator layer: Sustainability = entropy-weighted sum of normalized CO2 emissions, cultivated land area, water usage (weights W=[0.5213,0.2511,0.2276] from entropy method on 10 yrs data); Equity = Pop_hunger/Pop_sum; Profitability = sum(QB - PC)

Multi-objective optimization: Min = gamma*Equity/mean(Equity) + (1-gamma)*Sustainable/mean(Sustainable), s.t. Profit>0, Area>0, parameter positivity; dimensionless via division-by-mean; gamma swept 0->1 in 0.1 steps to trace the priority frontier
Solution method: MATLAB simulation of the coupled food system + Genetic Algorithm for approximate optima (used because Equity has no closed form and must be evaluated by simulation)
key_techniques: Three-stage pipeline architecture (production -> transportation -> sales) where each model's output feeds the next, so the indicators are emergent properties of a single coupled simulation rather than independent formulas

'Living area' abstraction: collapse tens of thousands of transporters/producers into one agent per province, with intra-area freight = 0 and uniform local price — the key tractability move that makes the whole simulation runnable
Calorie normalization with a single benchmark food (rice = 1) to make heterogeneous foods comparable and to tie production directly to the hunger metric
Entropy weight method to set sustainability sub-indicator weights objectively (data-driven) rather than picking weights by hand

Division-by-historical-mean to non-dimensionalize and combine two clashing objectives into one scalar, plus a single tunable trade-off knob gamma swept across [0,1] to produce a Pareto-style priority curve
Simulation-in-the-loop optimization: because Equity is only obtainable from the agent simulation, they wrap the whole MATLAB model inside a GA — treating the simulator as a black-box objective
Greedy profit-maximizing routing as a proxy for real market behavior (matches the problem's 'current systems prioritize efficiency/profitability' framing)

Mapping abstract model parameters back to real-world policy levers (k1/k2 -> transport subsidy/infrastructure; alpha -> price-volatility regulation; sigma -> wealth-gap reduction) to ground the optimization in actionable recommendations
why_it_won: "It directly and visibly answered every part of Problem E with a single coherent mechanism. The judges' rubric for Problem E rewards (1) a genuine SYSTEM model that produces all four required metrics, (2) concrete application to one developed + one developing country, (3) a quantified, time-resolved answer to 'what happens and how long', and (4) a trade-off analysis. This team nailed all four: the three coupled sub-models naturally emit efficiency/profitability/sustainability/equity as outputs (not bolted-on), India vs France gives a textbook developing/developed contrast with intuitive results (France exports surplus -> negative net calorie input; India hunger concentrated in high-transport-cost interior), the optimization yields specific numbers (France 37 months, India 43 months; India hungry drops 189.6M->150.5M), and the gamma-sweep plus 10-country generalization turns the benefit/cost question into a clean story (developing countries pay more cost / lose more profit when prioritizing equity+sustainability). The agent-based purchase/transport simulations read as 'close to real life', and they validated against reality (French self-sufficiency/export, Indian infrastructure gaps) which signals model credibility. The Somalia migration that BREAKS the model — then explains WHY (terrain not captured) and proposes a fix — is exactly the kind of self-aware adaptability analysis judges reward."
section_highlights: "Summary front-loads the whole arc (three models -> three indicators -> NLP optimization -> GA) and quotes headline numbers immediately (France 43mo / India 37mo, India -39.1M hungry) — judges can grade in 30 seconds. Two flowcharts up front: a simple food-cycle loop and a full model-overview DAG showing data flow between all sub-models. Notation table and explicit Assumptions-with-Justifications section signal modeling discipline. Each model section follows a clean pattern: motivation -> definition -> equations -> 'where' variable glossary, including a real-world motivating example (Japan: rice in Hokkaido vs population in Tokyo) to justify the transportation model. Algorithms are written as named, boxed pseudocode (Algorithm 1 & 2). The case study is heavily visualized — stacked bar of crop yields, calorie-production choropleths, net-calorie-input bar charts per state/region, hunger choropleths, before/after radar charts of crop mix, line charts for objective value/cost/benefit over time, and a 10-country scatter separating developed (upper-left) from developing (lower-right). Strengths/Weaknesses are honest and specific. Policy suggestions tie each model parameter to a concrete government action."
pitfalls_or_limitations: Heavy reliance on unvalidated parameters: k1, k2, alpha, beta, phi, sigma, rho are introduced but their calibration is hand-waved ('we query the data to determine them') — no sensitivity analysis on these despite them driving every result

Greedy single-step profit routing is myopic: each origin ships everything to the single most-profitable destination, which can starve all but one area and ignores capacity, perishability, and multi-destination splitting
Province-as-living-area assumes zero intra-province transport cost and uniform intra-province price — the team admits this hurts accuracy but never bounds the error
Straight-line capital-to-capital distance ignores terrain/roads — exactly the assumption that made the Somalia migration fail; it's a known weakness left mostly unfixed
Price model uses only supply/demand (no weather, policy shocks, storage, imports within the country) and demand is a crude linear beta*pop - phi*qb

The 'time to optimize' (37/43 months) treats all structural changes (re-allocating cropland, changing crop mix) as instantaneous in parameter space — the paper acknowledges real implementation lag but doesn't model it
Equity reduced to a single hunger headcount ratio; no distributional/regional fairness measure, no nutrition diversity beyond calories
GA gives only 'approximate optimal' with no report of convergence, seeds, or robustness; results could be local optima

Some figures are internally inconsistent / poorly labeled (radar 'before/after' fractions don't sum cleanly; France 43mo vs India 37mo in summary appears swapped vs the 37/43 in the time-curve section), which a stricter judge would flag
reusable_patterns: Decompose a 'system' problem into a sequential pipeline of coupled sub-models whose chained outputs naturally produce the required evaluation metrics — instead of writing a separate ad-hoc formula per metric

Use an aggregation/abstraction unit (here 'living area') with simplifying internal assumptions to collapse an intractable agent count into a runnable simulation; state the assumption and its justification explicitly
Normalize heterogeneous quantities to a single benchmark unit (calorie ratio vs rice=1) so different entities become comparable and tie directly to the target metric
Entropy weight method as a defensible, data-driven way to weight sub-indicators when you don't want to justify hand-picked weights

Combine conflicting objectives by dividing each by its historical mean (non-dimensionalize) then weighting with a single knob gamma, and SWEEP gamma across [0,1] to produce a trade-off/priority frontier rather than one point
Simulation-in-the-loop optimization: when a metric has no closed form, wrap the whole simulator as a black-box objective and drive it with a GA
Developed-vs-developing case pairing (India/France) plus an N-country generalization scatter to show the model generalizes and reveals a structural pattern

Stress-test the model on a deliberately hard/edge case (Somalia), let it FAIL, diagnose the missing mechanism (terrain), and propose the targeted fix — this demonstrates adaptability far more convincingly than only showing successes
Map every free model parameter back to a real-world policy lever in the recommendations section, so the math has direct decision-making payoff
Front-load headline numerical results in the Summary so judges can grade the deliverable immediately
